message.content:
你是獨立驗證者（與發現此問題的 reviewer 不同 context）。盡力反駁以下 finding 是真問題——它可能是誤報。
若你能證明它不是問題（誤讀程式碼 / 已被別處處理 / 不在執行路徑），判 isReal=false。若反駁失敗，判 isReal=true。
Finding：{"severity":"medium","where":"assets/Script/GameState/UnshowPrepareState.ts:247 HandleRespinGame()","problem":"本次改動刪除了唯一呼叫點（:99-103 原 if(IsRespinGame) HandleRespinGame()），使 HandleRespinGame() 成為 0 呼叫者的 orphan。專案 §10.5 規定『你的改動造成的 orphan → 刪除』，但作者以註解『暫留供 BC-8 實測接線』保留，違反自家死碼立場。grep 確認 assets/Script 內無其他呼叫者。","fix":"依 §10.5 刪除 HandleRespinGame() 方法本體（BC-8 上線時若需要再從 git 還原）；或若團隊確認 unshow Respin 恢復路徑近期必接線，則在追溯中明確標記為『暫緩刪除的本次 orphan』並取得用戶確認，不要靜默保留。","lens":"repro+quality（可重現性 + 質量）"}
審查目標：P1 Respin 雙層索引重構（含 FeatureWheel→FreeGame 真實回包驗證的前置重構）。

設計規格：.claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md（對照 §5 模組職責、§7 BC-x、§8 因果鏈）。
真實 server 回包：.claude_temp/respin_and_freegame.json（14 局：Round[0]=MG 2 plate 觸發 FG、Round[1..13]=FG 內含 respin、retrigger）。

審查範圍 = `git diff bab1dbd~2 HEAD` 的核心變更 + 未 commit 變更（`git diff HEAD` + 未追蹤 tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts）。

核心檔（自行用 git/Read/Grep 探索，勿逐行窮舉清單）：
- assets/Script/Respin/RespinPlateLogic.ts（純函式，BC-1/2/3 對應 tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts）
- assets/Script/GameView.ts（雙層游標 m_curSubPlateIndex、旗標 getter 化、m_respinLockedSet）
- assets/Script/GameState/{CheckState,EnterRespinState,LeaveRespinState,SpinState,RoundEndState,UnshowPrepareState,ScatterShowState,AwardState}.ts
- assets/Script/{RecoverSpinAck,EffectPlate,SlotReels}.ts

重點關注（不限於此）：
1. 因果鏈完整性——雙層化後 round 級資料 per-手重讀是否有遺漏點（規格 §6 矩陣外的漏網）。
2. 真實資料牴觸規格 P-3：RespinLockedPos 實含 col0/5（規格原假設只 col1-4）。EnterRespinState 的 EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC 開關處理是否正確、有無雙重鎖定/顯示錯亂。
3. ENTER_RESPIN→SPIN 繞過 ROUND_END 的 V-1 風險（framework roundController 放行）是否在碼上有兜底或隱患。
4. getter 投影（IsGoingToFree/IsGoingToRespinGame）在 FG 內 respin（Round[1..13]）情境語意是否正確。

驗證基線：tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts 30/30 pass；TS 5.4.5 下 assets/Script 0 錯誤（基線 10）。
用 Read/Grep 實際查證程式碼，不要憑空判斷。

attachment.content:
- huashu-slides: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。
- memory-to-skill: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用
- ms-agent-scheduled-prompts: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用
- ms-agent-text-token-signaling: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用
- ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用
- ms-portable-skill-authoring: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用
- ms-streaming-token-pipeline: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.
- ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.
- ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用
- ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用
- ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用
- skill-creator: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.
- uk-slot-extrabet: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。
- uk-slot-fake-reel-manager: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充
- uk-slot-multilang-sync: 把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的 gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到 「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、 或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架 (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼); 商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。
- uk-slot-state-machine: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。
- vc-kiro-delegate: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dev:init-claude: dev:init-claude — 開發設計閉環初始化
- sc:analyze: Comprehensive code analysis across quality, security, performance, and architecture domains
- sc:brainstorm: Interactive requirements discovery through Socratic dialogue and systematic exploration
- sc:build: Build, compile, and package projects with intelligent error handling and optimization
- sc:business-panel: /sc:business-panel - Business Panel Analysis System
- sc:cleanup: Systematically clean up code, remove dead code, and optimize project structure
- sc:design: Design system architecture, APIs, and component interfaces with comprehensive specifications
- sc:document: Generate focused documentation for components, functions, APIs, and features
- sc:estimate: Provide development estimates for tasks, features, or projects with intelligent analysis
- sc:explain: Provide clear explanations of code, concepts, and system behavior with educational clarity
- sc:git: Git operations with intelligent commit messages and workflow optimization
- sc:help: List all available /sc commands and their functionality
- sc:implement: Feature and code implementation with intelligent persona activation and MCP integration
- sc:improve: Apply systematic improvements to code quality, performance, and maintainability
- sc:index: Generate comprehensive project documentation and knowledge base with intelligent organization
- sc:load: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for project context loading
- sc:reflect: Task reflection and validation using Serena MCP analysis capabilities
- sc:save: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for session context persistence
- sc:select-tool: Intelligent MCP tool selection based on complexity scoring and operation analysis
- sc:spawn: Meta-system task orchestration with intelligent breakdown and delegation
- sc:spec-panel: Multi-expert specification review and improvement using renowned specification and software engineering experts
- sc:task: Execute complex tasks with intelligent workflow management and delegation
- sc:test: Execute tests with coverage analysis and automated quality reporting
- sc:troubleshoot: Diagnose and resolve issues in code, builds, deployments, and system behavior
- sc:workflow: Generate structured implementation workflows from PRDs and feature requirements
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dev-design: 架構設計 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 1+1b）：多方案 judge-panel → 對抗驗證砍缺陷 → 設計規格（含 BC-x）。承重核（因果鏈+事實求證）注入每個 agent。
- dev-prd: PRD / 需求探索 workflow（新能力，原方法論無 PRD 階段）：多角度需求探索（problem/user/scope）→ 塑形候選（lean/complete）→ 對抗挑戰 → PRD 文件。事實求證確保需求前提不是憑空假設。
- dev-review: 品質+安全審查 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 3）：parallel 多視角審查（correctness/security/repro lens）→ 對抗驗證 findings。異源 skeptic 打破同源自審天花板（#007）。
- dev-verify: 跨產出物自證 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 5，可選）：可枚舉項用 adversarial-verify；反向遍歷（找死碼/未實作）用輕量 verifier agent（這是 adversarial-verify 結構上蓋不到的缺口）。
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-mem:do: Execute a plan using subagents for implementation
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create an implementation plan with documentation discovery
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- webwright:craft: Craft a reusable Webwright CLI tool by parameterizing a web task.
- webwright:run: Run a one-shot web task with the Webwright Playwright workflow.
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- telegram:access: Manage Telegram channel access — approve pairings, edit allowlists, set DM/group policy. Use when the user asks to pair, approve someone, check who's allowed, or change policy for the Telegram channel.
- telegram:configure: Set up the Telegram channel — save the bot token and review access policy. Use when the user pastes a Telegram bot token, asks to configure Telegram, asks "how do I set this up" or "who can reach me," or wants to check channel status.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- webwright:webwright: Solve a user-specified web task code-as-action style by driving a local Playwright browser through one bash command at a time, saving screenshots and an action log into `final_runs/run_<id>/`, and visually verifying the result. Use when the user asks to automate a web task (search, filter, form-fill, multi-step flow, data extraction) and wants reusable scripts plus screenshot evidence rather than a one-shot answer.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the actual code changes and call paths.
The finding claims `HandleRespinGame()` is now an orphan (0 callers) due to the diff removing the only call site. Let me verify the diff and grep for callers.

input.command: git log --oneline -5 -- assets/Script/GameState/UnshowPrepareState.ts && echo "---DIFF---" && git diff bab1dbd~2 HEAD -- assets/Script/GameState/UnshowPrepareState.ts | head -120
input.description: Show git log and diff for UnshowPrepareState

content.text:
The facts are confirmed:

1. The diff at lines 99-103 removed the only call site `if (IsRespinGame) { this.HandleRespinGame(); }`.
2. Grep across `assets/Script` confirms `HandleRespinGame()` (the private method in UnshowPrepareState) has no remaining callers — the only other matches are comment references in the same file and an unrelated `HandleRespinGameAward` in AwardState.ts.
3. The method is `private`, so its scope is confined to this single file — no possibility of an external caller.
4. The author kept it with a comment `暫留供 BC-8 實測接線`.

Now let me assess whether the finding's claim that this *violates* §10.5 holds. The key question: is this a real problem (a genuine §10.5 violation that warrants action) or a false positive?

Let me re-read §10.5 precisely:

> - 你的改動造成的 orphan → **刪除**
> - 改動前已存在的 dead code → **提及，不動**
> - 用戶明確要求清理 → 才動

The method became an orphan *because of this change* (the call site was in the removed `if (IsRespinGame)` block). So it falls under the first bullet: "改動造成的 orphan → 刪除". The author instead retained it with a justification comment. Let me confirm the method was genuinely reachable before the change (i.e. it was live code, not pre-existing dead code).

Before the diff: the `else` branch set `IsRespinGame = true`, and then `if (this.m_gameView.IsRespinGame) { this.HandleRespinGame(); }` would fire. So pre-change it was reachable. Post-change both the setter and the call are gone, making it a newly-created orphan.

content.text:
This is a crucial discovery. There are TWO different "BC-8" in this codebase:

1. **P1 Respin spec BC-8** (P1-design-spec.md:324): "Unshow：UnshowStartRound 指多 plate 局 → 游標 (UnshowStartRound,0)、整局自 plate 0 重播並重入 ENTER_RESPIN；前提『server unshow 含完整 PlateQueue』待實測。" — This is the one the author's comment references.

The P1 spec explicitly designates BC-8 as `[framework-dependent·待實測]` (pending real-server testing), and the residual-risk section (§殘餘風險 items 2 and 5) explicitly notes:
- "全新路徑無回歸基線（Respin 流程目前生產不可達，舊 IsGoingToRespinGame 從未為 true）：BC-5~BC-8 無『改壞了』可比對象，只能對拍 server 樣本"
- "unshow 回包形狀未知（B-4/BC-8）：刪死碼分支後依賴 server 給完整 PlateQueue，待實測。"

So the design spec itself anticipates that the unshow-Respin recovery path is unverified and pending real-server samples. The author's decision to keep `HandleRespinGame()` with a clearly-documented BC-8 reference is a deliberate, spec-aligned "暫緩刪除" — and critically, it IS documented (in code comments referencing the tracked BC-8 item), not "靜默保留" (silently retained).

Now let me evaluate the finding's §10.5 argument carefully. The finding's own `fix` offers two options, the second being: "若團隊確認 unshow Respin 恢復路徑近期必接線，則在追溯中明確標記為『暫緩刪除的本次 orphan』並取得用戶確認，不要靜默保留。"

Let me check the tracing artifact to see if this orphan was explicitly tracked/disclosed.

.claude-loop\artifacts\P1-design-spec.md:336:| GameView.ts:1186/1224/1228/1237 | 賦值死碼 | 整段刪（5.2-4），tsc 把漏網變編譯錯誤 |

.claude-loop\artifacts\P1-design-spec.md:357:| ScatterShowState:288/898/965、SlotReels:2707、GameView:1007、UnshowPrepareState:78/87/92 | 「局首手正常盤面」或 sub=0 可達 | 保留 [0] + 逐點加註釋（發現 per-手可達者升級 [sub] 並記錄） |
.claude-loop\artifacts\P1-design-spec.md:365:- **呼叫者=0 警示**：HandleReel 刪除的 FreePlateSymbol/MainPlateSymbol——欄位 proto 不存在（A-3），不可能 inline 繞過，安全刪。UnshowPrepareState HandleRespinGame 若刪分支後呼叫者歸 0 → 一併刪（本改動造成的 orphan）。
.claude-loop\artifacts\P1-design-spec.md:385:5. **🟡 unshow 回包形狀未知**（B-4/BC-8）：刪死碼分支後依賴 server 給完整 PlateQueue，待實測。
.claude-loop\artifacts\P1-design-spec.md:407:4. GameView 雙層游標 + getter 化 + 刪死碼 → 編譯爆點全修（CheckState/EnterRespinState/FeatureShowController 賦值點）→ BC-4。

content.text:
This is the decisive evidence. The design spec is explicit and unambiguous on this exact point:

**P1-design-spec.md:365 (§因果鏈 呼叫者=0 警示):**
> "UnshowPrepareState HandleRespinGame 若刪分支後呼叫者歸 0 → **一併刪**（本改動造成的 orphan）。"

**P1-design-spec.md:253 (§5.7 修改說明):**
> "刪 :89-93 `CurrFeverGameType===RespinGame` 死碼分支（用戶確認；**其 HandleRespinGame 鎖列呼叫一併消失若 orphan**）"

So the design spec — which is the authoritative artifact the implementation must conform to, and which includes "用戶確認" (user confirmation) — explicitly prescribes that `HandleRespinGame()` **should be deleted** as "本改動造成的 orphan" (an orphan caused by this very change). This is exactly the §10.5 first bullet ("你的改動造成的 orphan → 刪除"), and the spec author already adjudicated it as a delete.

The implementation deviated from the spec: instead of deleting, the author retained the method with a "暫留供 BC-8 實測接線" comment. This is a genuine deviation from both (a) §10.5 of CLAUDE.md and (b) the binding design spec §因果鏈 directive that the user already confirmed.

Let me confirm there's no project-level convention that retaining documented future-hooks is acceptable, and verify TS still compiles (so the orphan is genuinely unreferenced, not a TS-error-masked situation).

input.command: git diff --stat HEAD -- assets/Script/GameState/UnshowPrepareState.ts; echo "--- is HandleRespinGame change committed or uncommitted? ---"; git log --oneline -1 -- assets/Script/GameState/UnshowPrepareState.ts
input.description: Check commit status of the orphan change

content.content:
--- is HandleRespinGame change committed or uncommitted? ---
f2f739e feat: Respin 雙層索引重構 — 結構化判斷旗標 + plate 內循環

content.text:
The change is committed in f2f739e (the P1 重構 commit). The orphan-creating change and its retention are both in committed code.

Let me do due diligence on the strongest counter-argument: maybe TS `noUnusedLocals`/private-unused detection would flag this, meaning the build is "clean" only because the project doesn't enforce unused-private detection — but regardless, the method being `private` and uncalled is definitionally dead code. The finding is about §10.5 policy compliance, not about a runtime bug. Let me verify the compile baseline is unaffected (confirming the orphan is truly unreferenced, not masking an error).

input.command: timeout 300 npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -i "UnshowPrepare\|HandleRespinGame" | head; echo "exit-grep done (empty = no errors referencing this file)"
input.description: Check tsc for errors referencing the orphan

content.content: exit-grep done (empty = no errors referencing this file)

content.text:
No compile errors reference the orphan — confirming `HandleRespinGame()` is genuinely uncalled and unreferenced (TS doesn't error on unused private methods by default, so it silently survives).

Let me now weigh the verdict carefully, steelmanning both sides.

**Case for false positive (reviewer misread / already handled):**
- The method retention IS documented with a clear comment referencing BC-8, not "靜默保留" (the finding's `problem` text says "違反自家死碼立場" but acknowledges the author left a comment).
- BC-8 is a real, tracked, `待實測` spec item — so the author has a colorable justification.

**Case for real problem:**
- §10.5 first bullet is unambiguous: "你的改動造成的 orphan → **刪除**". The diff demonstrably created the orphan (removed the call site at :99-103).
- The binding design spec **twice** explicitly adjudicated this exact method as "本改動造成的 orphan → 一併刪" (§5.7:253 and §因果鏈:365), and one of those carries "用戶確認".
- The implementation **deviated from its own confirmed spec** by keeping it.
- §10.5b's "提及，不動" exception applies only to *pre-existing* dead code — this is newly-created, so that exception does NOT apply.
- The finding's severity (medium) and lens (repro+quality) are appropriate: it's a maintainability/spec-conformance issue, not a runtime crash.

The "documented comment" mitigation does NOT neutralize the finding — it actually strengthens the case that this was a *conscious deviation* from a confirmed delete decision. The finding even anticipates this: its fix option 2 says if retention is desired, it must be "在追溯中明確標記為『暫緩刪除的本次 orphan』並取得用戶確認" — and there is no tracing artifact recording this retention as a deliberate, user-confirmed deviation (the only artifact, the spec, says to delete it).

I tried to refute the finding and failed. The orphan is real, newly-created by this diff, the spec explicitly mandated its deletion (with user confirmation), and the implementation deviated. This is a genuine §10.5 + spec-conformance violation.

One honest caveat affecting confidence: this is a **policy/maintainability** finding, not a functional bug — the retained dead method causes no runtime defect, and "keep a documented stub for an imminent feature" is a defensible engineering judgment some teams accept. So while the §10.5 letter is clearly violated, reasonable reviewers could rate it low rather than medium. That keeps it from being a slam-dunk on severity, but the core claim (real orphan, real policy violation, contradicts confirmed spec) is solid.

input.reasoning:
反駁失敗，finding 成立。

事實核實（Read + Grep + git diff）：
1. diff（commit f2f739e）在 UnshowPrepareState.ts:99-103 移除了唯一呼叫點 `if (IsRespinGame) { this.HandleRespinGame(); }`。
2. `HandleRespinGame()` 為 private 方法，grep 全 assets/Script 確認 0 呼叫者（其餘命中皆為同檔註解 + 無關的 AwardState.HandleRespinGameAward）。private 範圍封閉，不可能有外部呼叫者。
3. tsc 對該檔 0 錯誤，證實該方法確實無引用（TS 預設不報 unused private，靜默存活）——是貨真價實的 orphan。
4. 此 orphan 是「本次改動造成」（呼叫點隨 :89-93 的 RespinGame 分支一併被刪），非改動前既存死碼。∴ 適用 §10.5 第一條「你的改動造成的 orphan → 刪除」，不適用第二條「既存 dead code → 提及不動」。

關鍵反證（決定性）：本任務的 binding 設計規格 P1-design-spec.md 對此方法已兩處明確裁定為「刪除」：
- §5.7:253「刪 :89-93 死碼分支（用戶確認）；其 HandleRespinGame 鎖列呼叫一併消失若 orphan」
- §因果鏈:365「UnshowPrepareState HandleRespinGame 若刪分支後呼叫者歸 0 → 一併刪（本改動造成的 orphan）」
規格（含用戶確認）已判定刪除，實作卻以「暫留供 BC-8 實測接線」保留，屬對自家已確認規格 + §10.5 的雙重偏離。

對「�handled / 誤報」假說的反駁：作者留了 BC-8 註解，但 (a) 註解只是把保留理由寫進碼，不等於 §10.5 允許的處置；(b) finding 自己的 fix 選項 2 要求若要保留須「在追溯中明確標記為暫緩刪除的 orphan 並取得用戶確認」——而唯一追溯（規格）反而說刪，無任何 artifact 記錄此保留為用戶確認的刻意偏離。文檔化反而坐實這是有意識地違反已確認的刪除決策。

誠實 caveat（壓低 confidence 至 0.8）：此為政策/可維護性 finding 而非 runtime bug，保留的死方法不致功能缺陷；「為近期將接線的功能留一個有文檔的 stub」是部分團隊可接受的工程判斷，故 severity 評 medium 偏高、評 low 亦合理。但核心主張（真 orphan + 本次造成 + 違反 §10.5 + 牴觸已確認規格）證據紮實，isReal=true。

content.text:
已完成驗證。Finding 成立（isReal=true，confidence 0.8）。

核心結論：`HandleRespinGame()` 確實因本次 diff（f2f739e，移除 :99-103 的 `if(IsRespinGame) HandleRespinGame()` 呼叫點）變成 0 呼叫者的 orphan，且為「本次改動造成」非既存死碼，正中 §10.5 第一條「刪除」。決定性反證是 binding 設計規格 P1-design-spec.md 兩處（:253 含用戶確認、:365）已明確裁定此方法「一併刪（本改動造成的 orphan）」，實作卻保留——屬對已確認規格的偏離。BC-8 註解非但不能脫罪，反而坐實這是有意識違反確認過的刪除決策。

唯一壓低信心的點：這是政策/可維護性問題非 runtime bug，severity medium 偏高（low 亦合理），但核心主張紮實。
